overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted academic presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, with a total follower base of 3,580. His content effectively reaches researchers, students, and professionals in computational social science, generating solid engagement particularly around new publications, software releases like CooRnet, and academic career opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Research updates, paper publications, and event presentations on detecting coordinated networks and social media manipulation.
Job openings, postdoctoral roles, and PhD opportunities within the computational social science research team.
Announcements regarding major research grants and updates on open-source tools like CooRnet.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's highly active academic community by continuing to share student opportunities and publication drafts, as it yields the highest relative engagement rate.
Increase visual media usage on LinkedIn, such as slide decks or infographics, to capture more attention from its larger, professional audience.
Cross-promote major research tool updates across all three networks using platform-specific hashtags to capture niche technical audiences.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky currently delivers the highest average engagement rate (6.14%), indicating a highly receptive and dense network of academic peers who actively repost and interact with publication news.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides steady engagement (4.55% average ER) from a highly technical audience, making it ideal for software releases and methodological discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest audience, driving the highest absolute volume of interactions (likes and comments) for professional milestones such as grants and recruitment.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
